Guideline: Load Alpine with a deferred script
Description: The defer attribute ensures Alpine runs after the DOM is parsed so it can initialize components.
Do: Include the CDN build with defer in the head.
Don't: Load the script without defer, so Alpine runs before the DOM exists.

Guideline: Start every component with x-data
Description: x-data marks HTML as an Alpine component and is required on a parent for most directives to work.
Do: Declare reactive state in an x-data object on a parent element.
Don't: Use x-on, x-bind, x-model or x-show without an ancestor x-data.

Guideline: Reference component state with this inside x-data
Description: Methods and getters run as plain JavaScript, so component properties must be reached through this.
Do: Use this.property inside methods and getters.
Don't: Reference bare property names inside x-data methods.

Guideline: Put a single element's state on the element itself
Description: When a component is just one element, declaring x-data directly avoids an unnecessary wrapper.
Do: Declare x-data on the single element it applies to.
Don't: Wrap a lone element in an extra div just to hold x-data.

Guideline: Opt out of an expression for behavior-only components
Description: Alpine allows x-data with no value when you need component behavior but no reactive data.
Do: Use bare x-data (or x-data="{}") when there is no state.
Don't: Invent unused state just to satisfy x-data.

Guideline: Extract repeated markup before reaching for JS
Description: Alpine's first recommendation for reused components is a server-side template partial, then Alpine.data().
Do: Extract repeated blocks into a backend partial or Alpine.data().
Don't: Copy-paste large inline x-data objects across many elements.
Code Bad: <div x-data="{ open:false, toggle(){/*...*/} }"> <!-- duplicated many times -->

Guideline: Register reusable data on alpine:init
Description: Alpine.data() components must be registered before Alpine initializes the DOM.
Do: Register Alpine.data() inside the alpine:init event listener.
Don't: Register components after Alpine has already booted.

Guideline: Bind an object of directives with x-bind
Description: x-bind can apply a named set of directives to an element, keeping complex behavior in JS.
Do: Return a directive object from Alpine.data() and apply it with x-bind.
Don't: Scatter many inline directives when they belong to a reusable component.

Guideline: Use getters for derived state
Description: JS getters expose computed values as plain properties for a more expressive syntax.
Do: Define get filteredItems() and read it like a property.
Don't: Recompute the same derived value inline in multiple directives.
Code Good: <div x-data="{ items:[], search:'', get filtered(){ return this.items.filter(i=>i.startsWith(this.search)) } }">

Guideline: Avoid expensive work in frequently-read getters
Description: Alpine getters are not cached like Vue computed properties, so they run on every read.
Do: Keep getters cheap, or memoize costly derivations elsewhere.
Don't: Do heavy computation in a getter read by many directives.

Guideline: Set text with x-text, not innerHTML
Description: x-text sets textContent from an expression and escapes any markup safely.
Do: Use x-text for dynamic text content.
Don't: Reach for x-html when plain text will do.

Guideline: Two-way bind inputs with x-model
Description: x-model keeps an input and a data property in sync in both directions.
Do: Bind form fields with x-model on a component with x-data.
Don't: Manually wire @input handlers to mirror value into state.

Guideline: Cast typed values with .number or .boolean
Description: x-model stores values as strings by default, so use modifiers when a typed value is needed.
Do: Add .number or .boolean to store the correct JS type.
Don't: Compare a numeric input against a number while it holds a string.
Code Bad: <input type="text" x-model="age"> <!-- age is a string -->

Guideline: Delay validation-heavy updates with .lazy
Description: x-model.lazy syncs on the change event, ideal for holding off real-time validation until blur.
Do: Use x-model.lazy when you want to validate after the field loses focus.
Don't: Fire validation on every keystroke via the default binding.

Guideline: Seed inputs from server values with .fill
Description: x-model ignores the value attribute by default; .fill lets it populate an empty property.
Do: Add .fill so server-rendered value attributes seed empty state.
Don't: Expect the value attribute to hydrate x-model without .fill.

Guideline: Collect grouped checkboxes into an array
Description: Multiple checkboxes bound to the same property build an array of their values.
Do: Bind grouped checkboxes to one array property.
Don't: Track each checkbox with a separate boolean when they are one group.
Code Good: <input type="checkbox" value="red" x-model="colors"><input type="checkbox" value="blue" x-model="colors">

Guideline: Prevent default with the .prevent modifier
Description: The .prevent modifier calls preventDefault(), commonly to stop native form submission.
Do: Use @submit.prevent to handle a form in Alpine.
Don't: Call $event.preventDefault() by hand when a modifier exists.

Guideline: Throttle high-frequency events, debounce settling ones
Description: Use .throttle for continuous events like scroll and .debounce for input that should fire once activity stops.
Do: Use @scroll.throttle for scroll and @input.debounce for search.
Don't: Debounce scroll (never fires) or hit the network on every keystroke.

Guideline: Close menus with @click.outside
Description: The .outside modifier fires when a click lands outside the element and only while it is visible.
Do: Use @click.outside to dismiss dropdowns and popovers.
Don't: Wire manual document listeners and visibility checks by hand.
Code Bad: <div x-show="open" @click.window="open=false"> <!-- closes on its own toggle -->

Guideline: Register page-wide listeners with .window
Description: The .window modifier attaches the listener to window for global events like Escape.
Do: Use @keyup.escape.window for page-level shortcuts.
Don't: Add manual window.addEventListener calls in x-init instead.

Guideline: Keep event names lowercase in the attribute
Description: HTML attributes are case-insensitive, so camelCase event names must use the .camel modifier.
Do: Use kebab-case with .camel to target a camelCased event.
Don't: Write @customEvent expecting a camelCase listener.

Guideline: Mark touch listeners .passive for scroll performance
Description: Omitting .passive on touch listeners can block the browser's scroll optimizations.
Do: Add .passive to touch event listeners.
Don't: Attach touchstart handlers without .passive.

Guideline: Declare x-for on a template with one root
Description: x-for must live on a <template> element containing exactly one root element.
Do: Wrap loop content in a single-root <template x-for>.
Don't: Put x-for on a normal element or use multiple roots.

Guideline: Add a stable :key to iterated lists
Description: Keys let Alpine track elements across reorders, adds, and removes to avoid odd side-effects.
Do: Bind :key to a unique, stable id in x-for.
Don't: Omit :key, or key by array index, on reorderable lists.

Guideline: Wrap x-if content in a single-root template
Description: x-if adds and removes real DOM and must be declared on a <template> with one root element.
Do: Put x-if on a <template> wrapping one root element.
Don't: Apply x-if directly to an element or with multiple roots.

Guideline: Choose x-show for frequent toggles
Description: x-show only flips CSS display and stays in the DOM, so it is cheap to toggle repeatedly and supports transitions.
Do: Use x-show for elements toggled often or that need transitions.
Don't: Use x-if for a frequently toggled element, paying re-create cost each time.

Guideline: Choose x-if for rarely rendered markup
Description: x-if keeps markup out of the DOM until needed, avoiding the initial cost of elements that may never show.
Do: Use x-if for heavy or seldom-shown content.
Don't: Render expensive rarely-used markup with x-show, keeping it in the DOM.

Guideline: Prefer an init() method for setup logic
Description: Alpine automatically calls an init() method on x-data, which is cleaner than cramming logic into inline x-init.
Do: Define init() in x-data for non-trivial startup work.
Don't: Stuff multi-statement setup into an inline x-init string.

Guideline: Await async setup inside x-init
Description: x-init supports await, so data can be fetched into state before further DOM processing.
Do: Fetch and assign async data with await in x-init or init().
Don't: Fetch without awaiting and read the data before it resolves.

Guideline: Wait for render with $nextTick
Description: Code needing the finished DOM should run inside $nextTick, since plain x-init runs before rendering completes.
Do: Wrap post-render work in $nextTick(() => ...).
Don't: Read rendered dimensions directly in x-init before render finishes.

Guideline: Toggle classes with the object syntax
Description: x-bind class object syntax maps class names to booleans and does not persist toggled classes in the attribute.
Do: Use :class object syntax to toggle preset classes.
Don't: Concatenate class strings manually for conditional classes.

Guideline: Rely on :class preserving existing classes
Description: String/ternary :class bindings merge with, rather than overwrite, an element's existing classes.
Do: Combine static class and :class knowing existing classes persist.
Don't: Assume :class wipes the element's static classes.

Guideline: Use $refs instead of document.querySelector
Description: $refs is a scoped, succinct alternative for manually reaching DOM elements within a component.
Do: Tag elements with x-ref and access them via $refs.
Don't: Use global document.querySelector from inside a component.

Guideline: Do not create refs dynamically inside x-for
Description: In V3 $refs only resolves for statically-created elements; a bound :x-ref stays a literal string.
Do: Reference static elements with $refs; use data for dynamic items.
Don't: Bind :x-ref inside an x-for loop expecting a live reference.

Guideline: Communicate between components with $dispatch and .window
Description: Dispatched events bubble to common ancestors, so cross-component and sibling listeners need .window.
Do: Listen with .window when dispatcher and listener are siblings or separate components.
Don't: Listen for a dispatched event on a sibling without .window.

Guideline: Pass event payloads via detail
Description: $dispatch sends a second-argument payload that Alpine exposes on $event.detail.
Do: Read dispatched data from $event.detail.
Don't: Try to read the payload from the event object directly.

Guideline: Reserve Alpine.store for truly page-wide state
Description: Global stores are meant for data shared across every component; local x-data and Alpine.data() come first.
Do: Use $store only for genuinely global state like theme.
Don't: Default to global stores for state a single component owns.
Code Bad: Alpine.store('dropdownOpen', false) // belongs in local x-data

Guideline: Register stores on alpine:init
Description: Stores must be registered before components initialize so $store is available at boot.
Do: Declare Alpine.store() inside the alpine:init listener.
Don't: Register a store after Alpine has already initialized components.

Guideline: Prevent flash of uninitialized content with x-cloak
Description: x-cloak hides elements until Alpine loads and strips the attribute, avoiding a raw-template blip.
Do: Add x-cloak plus the [x-cloak]{display:none!important} CSS.
Don't: Ship dynamic content with no x-cloak and let raw markup flash.

Guideline: Never feed user content to x-html
Description: x-html sets innerHTML and rendering untrusted content opens XSS vulnerabilities.
Do: Use x-html only for content you fully control.
Don't: Bind user-provided or third-party HTML with x-html.
